event_text: The Habsburgs inherit the crowns of Bohemia and Hungary after the Battle of Mohács.
The Great Turkish War pushes Ottoman power back from Central Europe.
The Pragmatic Sanction sets out Habsburg succession through the female line.
Maria Theresa inherits the Habsburg monarchy.
The Austrian Empire is proclaimed.
Revolutions and national uprisings shake the Habsburg Empire.
Austria is defeated by Prussia, weakening its influence in German affairs.
The Austro-Hungarian Compromise creates Austria-Hungary as a Dual Monarchy.
The Croatian-Hungarian Settlement defines Croatia-Slavonia's position within the Hungarian half of the monarchy.
Austria-Hungary occupies Bosnia and Herzegovina after the Congress of Berlin.
Austria-Hungary and Germany form the Dual Alliance.
The Triple Alliance links Austria-Hungary, Germany and Italy.
Hungary marks the millennium of the Hungarian state.
Universal male suffrage is introduced in the Austrian half of the monarchy.
Austria-Hungary formally annexes Bosnia and Herzegovina.
Archduke Franz Ferdinand is assassinated in Sarajevo.
Austria-Hungary declares war on Serbia, helping start the First World War.
Emperor Franz Joseph dies after a reign of nearly 68 years.
Austria-Hungary collapses and successor states emerge across Central Europe.
The Treaty of Saint-Germain confirms the break-up of the Austrian half of the monarchy.
The Treaty of Trianon redraws Hungary's borders after the collapse of Austria-Hungary.
